---
title: "About"
pagetitle: "The Footy Forest | About"
toc: true
---

::: {.content}

## The model

The Footy Forest is an ensemble machine learning system that predicts AFL match outcomes. It combines five different classification algorithms into a single stacked model using the [tidymodels](https://www.tidymodels.org/) framework in R.

### Ensemble composition

The model stack is built using the [stacks](https://stacks.tidymodels.org/) package, which blends predictions from tuned candidate models via penalised logistic regression (LASSO). The contributing algorithms are:

- **Random Forest** (ranger) — captures non-linear interactions between predictors
- **Support Vector Machine** (kernlab) — finds optimal decision boundaries in high-dimensional space
- **GLMnet** — regularised logistic regression for stable, interpretable predictions
- **Naive Bayes** — probabilistic classifier that handles predictor distributions
- **Neural Network** (nnet) — single hidden layer MLP for flexible pattern recognition

The relative weighting of each model varies from round to round as the stack is retrained on the latest data. Model composition is tracked and logged each week.

### Predictor variables

Four scaled differentials (home minus away, rescaled to -100 to +100) drive the predictions:

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| **Power** | ELO rating differential. Higher means the home team has a stronger overall record. |
| **Venue effect** | Performance at the specific venue relative to ELO expectations, estimated from 5 years of data with Bayesian shrinkage. |
| **Team rating** | Average player rating differential based on selected lineups (from AFL Player Ratings). Shows as TBA until teams are announced. |
| **Travel** | Haversine distance differential in km between each team's home base and the match venue. |


### Training and validation

The model trains on all regular-season matches from 2015 onward (excluding finals). Validation uses a time-based split: training on pre-2023 data and testing on 2023–2025 seasons. Hyperparameters are tuned via rolling-origin cross-validation within the training set.

### ELO system

The underlying ELO ratings use the following parameters:

- **K-factor**: 60
- **Season carry-over**: 70% (30% regression to 1000 each season)
- **Home ground advantage**: 0 (estimated separately via venue effects)
- **Margin capping**: ±80 points

## Data sources

- Match results, fixtures, and player stats via the [fitzRoy](https://jimmyday12.github.io/fitzRoy/) R package (AFL API)
- Team and venue coordinates for travel calculations
- Player ratings from AFL's official rating points

## Who made this

The Footy Forest is built and maintained by Matt Nurse — a researcher, communicator, and AFL fan based in Canberra. The site is built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org) and hosted on GitHub Pages.

## Source code

The prediction pipeline and this website are open source. Find the code on [GitHub](https://github.com/yourusername/footyforest).

:::
